section.heading: Purpose
section.chapeau: The purpose of this subpart is to improve students’ academic achievement by increasing the capacity of States, local educational agencies, schools, and local communities to—

paragraph.content: provide all students with access to a well-rounded education;
improve school conditions for student learning; and
improve the use of technology in order to improve the academic achievement and digital literacy of all students.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 89–10, title IV, § 4101, as added Pub. L. 114–95, title IV, § 4101, Dec. 10, 2015, 129 Stat. 1968.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 7111, Pub. L. 89–10, title IV, § 4111, as added Pub. L. 107–110, title IV, § 401, Jan. 8, 2002, 115 Stat. 1735, related to reservations and allotments, prior to the general amendment of this subpart by Pub. L. 114–95.

Another prior section 7111, Pub. L. 89–10, title IV, § 4011 [4111], as added Pub. L. 103–382, title I, § 101, Oct. 20, 1994, 108 Stat. 3674, related to reservations and allotments, prior to the general amendment of this subchapter by Pub. L. 107–110.
A prior section 4101 of Pub. L. 89–10 was classified to section 3061 of this title prior to the general amendment of Pub. L. 89–10 by Pub. L. 103–382.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Dec. 10, 2015, except with respect to certain noncompetitive programs and competitive programs, see section 5 of Pub. L. 114–95, set out as an Effective Date of 2015 Amendment note under section 6301 of this title.
